titlestmt.titleproper: James Cox-Stevens Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments
Cox-Stevens (James) Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans and Diane Ducharme

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 5, 2022 at 04:04

did.unittitle: James Stevens-Cox collection of manuscript leaves and fragments

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Almost entirely in Latin; one item in Middle French.

did.abstract: The James Stevens-Cox Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments consists of 45 items, some full individual leaves and others cuttings of illuminated initials or decorative elements, dating from between approximately 1250 and approximately 1525. Contents include: a full leaf from a French bible, possibly produced in Liessies circa 1250, with five decorated initials; a calendar leaf from a book of hours by the Dunois Master; 6 leaves, five with initials, from the Bohun Bible; and many leaves containing historiated initials from bibles, books of hours and service books, mostly French or Italian.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Maggs Brothers on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2020.

bioghist.head: James Stevens-Cox (1900-1997)
bioghist.p: James Stevens-Cox (1900-1997) was the son of a wigmaker and hairdresser. Educated at Bristol Grammar School, he left at 16 to join the family business. Around 1930, he purchased the 75,000 volume stock of a second-hand book dealer in Bristol and opened the Coleridge Bookshop, which he developed into a major antiquarian bookstore. Stevens-Cox also authored several volumes of local history and archaeology, collected early manuscripts and fragments, and kept bees and birds. He died in 1997.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Stevens-Cox Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments. General Collection, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Descriptions of individual items are based on a list received with the collection, edited to conform with current descriptive standards.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The James Stevens-Cox Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments consists of 45 items, some full individual leaves and others cuttings of illuminated initials or decorative elements, dating from between approximately 1250 and approximately 1525. Contents include: a full leaf from a French bible, possibly produced in Liessies circa 1250, with five decorated initials; a calendar leaf from a book of hours by the Dunois Master; 6 leaves, five with initials, from the Bohun Bible; and many leaves containing historiated initials from bibles, books of hours and service books, mostly French or Italian.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The James Stevens-Cox Collection of Manuscript Leaves and Fragments is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropraite curator.

did.unittitle: Epistolae, by St. Gregory, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand. 2 cols. decorated with five large initials. Rubricated.

Historia ecclesiastica, manuscript fragment, from Rufinus' supplement to Eusebius, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 1 of 2 cols, 37 lines, ruled in plummet, comprising the end of Book X, a capitula list of 35 chapters, and the beginning of Book XI, decorated with a 9 line initial and with a 16 line descender, incorporating a dragon and two lions.

Vita S. Livini, by Boniface, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, preserving 14 lines of one column and part of another, pricked in the inner margin and ruled in plummet, written above top line in a late romanesque script, the recto comprising the start of the prologue, with 11 line illuminated foliate initial, the verso with the start of the Vita.

Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with Baruch in a historiated initial. 2 cols. of 49 lines, comprising Lamentations 4:2-Baruch 2:10, including the Prayer of Jeremiah and the usual prologue to Baruch, illuminated with a historiated initial depicting Baruch writing, another initial with a bird, flourished initials with extensions, and prickings in three outer margins and guides to the rubricator.
Bible, manuscript leaf from the Interpretations of Hebrew Names from Avuam to Baliada with illuminated intial, 2 cols. of 49 lines, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand.

Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with St. Paul in an historiated intial, painted by an illuminator in the circle of the Master of Bagnacavallo and the Master of Imola with a four-line foliate initial.

Decretals of Gregory IX, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, consisting of part of Book I, c.111-115, with 4 flourished initials, and with glosses in several unidentified hands.

Decretals of Gregory IX, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, comprising parts of Book III from the end of tit.13 to the middle of tit.17, tit. 14, with an illuminated historiated initial of a king's head at the start of the section De commodato, with glosses in unidentified hands.

Antiphoner [Antiphonary], manuscript fragment, 7 lines of text and music comprising the chant for Matins for the summer Sundays De Job, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with a historiated initial of Job.
Book of Hours, Use of Sarum, manuscript fragment with Zodiac signs and occupations of the months on a calendar leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand.
Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, comprising Obadiah 1:12-Jonah 1;16, with two standard prologues, and with Jonah in a historiated initial.

Psalter-Hymnal, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with part of 9 lines of text from the common of martyrs, and St. Catherine in a historiated initial by an artist in the circle of Lodovico de Gaçis.

Psalter-Hymnal, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with 9 lines of text, and God the Father in a historiated initial, illuminated by an artist in the circle of Lodovico de Gaçis.
Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 2 cols., 41 lines, comprising Numbers 21:4-23:4, and two chapter initials

Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 2 cols., 41 lines comprising the end of a capitula list, the standard Paris prologue, and Romans 1:1-32, with pre-modern chapter divisions at 1:8, 1:13, and 1:26, decorated with a 6-line foliate initial and a 7-line historiated initial depicting St. Paul holding a sword and book, painted by an illuminator of the circle of the Master of Bagnacavallo and the Master of Imola.

Bible, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, written in 2 cols. of 47 lines, comprising Leviticus 26:1-27:27, decorated with 2 illuminated initials each with a border and a cadel incorporating a human head.
Bible, manuscript leaves, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, written in 2 cols. of 22 lines.
Book of Hours, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, 16 lines, comprising the end of Terce and rubric for Sext, with 4 large and two small illuminated initials, pencil foliation 48,

Book of Hours, Use of Sarum, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 17 lines, comprising part of Compline, with 3 illustrated 3-line initials to the capitulum, Nunc dimittis, and Psalm 130, with pencil note, English c. 1380.

Book of Hours, manuscript leaves, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 15 lines, numbered in pencil 17 and 18 comprising a prayer and rubric for a prayer to God the Father, and the last lines of the prayer Ecclesiam tuam,followed by an illuminated line-filler.

Book of Hours, manuscript leaves, on parchment, in Dutch, in unidentified hand, 19 lines, foliated in pencil 23, 176, and 179 and numbered 9, 10, 11, including a 2-line illuminated initial at the beginning of the Te Deum, and numerous 1-line initials, part of a prayer (deliken intfaen...) and the final lines of a prayer (dier iuwen...), with an illuminated line-filler.

Book of Hours, manuscript leaves, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with illuminated initials at the beginning of Psalm 26, the Psalms said at Matins on Wednesday and Saturdays, and the first nocturn in the Office of the Dead.
Book of Hours, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in French, in unidentified hand, comprising part of the Fifteen Joys of the Virgin, illuminated with 5 initials and 5 line-fillers.
Book of Hours, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, comprising the end of the gospel extract from Mark and the beginning of John with a 2-line initial.
Decretals of Gregory IX, glossed, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, illuminated initial, 2 cols. of 22 lines comprising part of Book I, tit.4-6.

Decretals of Gregory IX, glossed, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, part of 2 cols. of 11 lines, comprising Book III, tit.5-6, with a king's head on historiated initial.
Bible, alphabetical concordance, letters o-p, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with two human-animal hybrid illustrations.

Rationale divinorum officiorum, by Guilielmus Durandus, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 2 cols. of 45 lines, comprising the end of Book IV and the beginning of Book V, and illuminated foliate initial.
Quadragesimale, by Johannes Gritsch, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand.

Missal, manuscript leaf, parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 2 cols. of 29 lines with illuminated initial at the beginning of the Office for the Virgin, inscribed "Dyson Perrins" and "French c.1345."
Missal, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, with initials.

Breviary, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 2 cols. of 24 lines, comprising parts of the 9th to 12th lessons at Matins and the beginning of Lauds for an unidentified feast day, with 4 illuminated initials, numbered in pencil 52.

Breviary, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, innermost column of 2 cols., 34 lines, with music, comprising part of the text and music for the 4th Sunday after the Octave of Easter.
Lectionary, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 27 lines comprising 1 Samuel 2:18-3:1, with ink foliation 198 and modern pencil number 2.
Lectionary, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 18 lines, comprising readings for martyrs in the Common of Saints with two 2-line initials and foliation 174.
Choirbook, manuscript fragment, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, two men's faces in a cadel initial with text and music in square notation on a 4-line red stave.
Choirbook, manuscript leaft,on parchment, in Latin, 7 lines of text and music in square notation, comprising chants for the Common of Saints, with 5 initials.

Antiphoner ; [Antiphonary], manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 8 lines of text and music, including the chant for Thursday to Saturday for the third week of Advent, with seven initials, with pencil foliation and ink pagination.

Antiphoner [Antiphonary], manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 5 lines of text and music in square notation, including the chants for Laudes at Easter, foliation 36 in margin.
Antiphoner [Antiphonary], manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, 5 lines of text and music in square notation, including the chant for Pentecost, 2 flourished initials, foliation 99.

Antiphoner [Antiphonary], manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 5 lines of text and music in square notation, including chant for the Common of Saints, 3 flourished initials, ink foliation 125.
Gradual, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 8 lines of square musical notation, illuminated initial, introduces the introit for the first Sunday after Ascension.
Gradual, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 7 lines of text and music in square notation, from Passion Sunday, foliation cxliij.
Gradual, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 7 lines of text and music in square notation, chant for Tuesday in Holy Week, with 3 initials and foliation clv.

Gradual, manuscript leaf, on parchment, in Latin, in unidentified hand, 7 lines of text and music in square notation including the chant for Wednesday in the 4th week of Lent, initials, ink foliation lxxiiij.
